morphology.summary: A learned compound-like form built from return-home and pain elements.

etymology.summary: The word is built from Greek elements for homecoming and pain, historically used for homesickness.

semantic_drift.summary: The meaning shifts from homesickness or pathological longing to sentimental longing for a past time or state.

developments.description: The compound originally frames longing as a pain of return.
The target of longing broadens from home to remembered time, culture, or self.

present_usage.summary: Modern nostalgia is usually emotional longing for the past, sometimes comforting and sometimes distorting.

senses.definition: A wistful or sentimental longing for a past place, time, experience, or state.

examples.text: The song filled the room with nostalgia for a summer none of them could repeat.
examples.note: Original EI usage example for this capsule.

senses.definition: An idealizing attachment to the past that may obscure its difficulties.

examples.text: The campaign traded on nostalgia for a simpler national story.
examples.note: Original EI usage example for this capsule.

cultural_frame.summary: The word appears in personal memory, marketing, politics, and cultural criticism.

frames.label: memory comfort
frames.description: Frames the past as emotionally sustaining.

frames.label: selective past
frames.description: Frames the past as idealized or politically mobilized.

pragmatics.summary: Nostalgia can console, sell, romanticize, or warn about selective memory.

stances.label: comfort
stances.description: Presents longing as warm or meaningful.

stances.label: critique
stances.description: Presents longing as selective or regressive.

expansion.paragraph: Nostalgia combines the ideas of return and pain, originally framing homesickness as a kind of suffering. Modern usage usually broadens that longing from home to the remembered past. Its stance varies: nostalgia can be tender comfort, commercial atmosphere, or a warning about idealized memory.

trace.contribution: The word is built from Greek elements for homecoming and pain, historically used for homesickness.
The meaning shifts from homesickness or pathological longing to sentimental longing for a past time or state.
Nostalgia can console, sell, romanticize, or warn about selective memory.

capsule_summary: A return-plus-pain word whose meaning broadened from homesickness to longing for a remembered or imagined past.
